abstract: Initializes a path using the positions of the specified graph nodes.
overview:
## Return Value

A new path object.

## Discussion

Use this initializer to turn a list of nodes from a navigation graph (as returned by the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph] [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method) into a path-following goal for an agent. If the nodes are [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode2D] objects, this initializer creates a 2D path; if the nodes are [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode3D] objects, this initializer creates a 3D path.

The `radius` parameter defines the space occupied by the path—think of this space as the area created by sweeping a circle (or sphere, for 3D paths) of the specified radius along the path from vertex to vertex. Agents with path-related goals will attempt to move to or stay within this area.

To use the newly created path to constrain an agent’s behavior, create a goal with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGoal/init(toStayOn:maxPredictionTime:)] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGoal/init(toFollow:maxPredictionTime:forward:)] method.

# init(graphNodes:radius:)

**Initializer**

Initializes a path using the positions of the specified graph nodes.

## Declaration

```swift
init(graphNodes: [GKGraphNode], radius: Float)
```

## Parameters

- **graphNodes**: An array of graph node objects containing 2D points.
- **radius**: The radius of the path.

## Return Value

A new path object.

## Discussion

Use this initializer to turn a list of nodes from a navigation graph (as returned by the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph] [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method) into a path-following goal for an agent. If the nodes are [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode2D] objects, this initializer creates a 2D path; if the nodes are [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode3D] objects, this initializer creates a 3D path.

The `radius` parameter defines the space occupied by the path—think of this space as the area created by sweeping a circle (or sphere, for 3D paths) of the specified radius along the path from vertex to vertex. Agents with path-related goals will attempt to move to or stay within this area.

To use the newly created path to constrain an agent’s behavior, create a goal with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGoal/init(toStayOn:maxPredictionTime:)] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGoal/init(toFollow:maxPredictionTime:forward:)] method.
title: init(graphNodes:radius:)
